17.gl: a particle of affirmation, surely; hence (by limitation) only
17.kj: also, in any wise, at least, but, certainly, even, howbeit, nevertheless, notwithstanding, only, save, surely, of a surety, truly, verily, [phrase] wherefore, yet (but).

18.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
18.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

18.gl: a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term (and in such cases frequently not expressed in translation)
18.kj: also, another, any (man), a certain, [phrase] champion, consent, each, every (one), fellow, (foot-, husband-) man, (good-, great, mighty) man, he, high (degree), him (that is), hus
18.dv: contracted for H582 (אֱנוֹשׁ) (or perhaps rather from an unused root meaning to be extant);

18.gl: to inherit (as a (figurative) mode of descent), or (generally) to occupy; causatively, to bequeath, or (generally) distribute, instate
18.kj: divide, have (inheritance), take as a heritage, (cause to, give to, make to) inherit, (distribute for, divide (for, for an, by), give for, have, leave for, take (for)) inheritance,

18.gl: properly, something inherited, i.e. (abstractly) occupancy, or (concretely) an heirloom; generally an estate, patrimony or portion
18.kj: heritage, to inherit, inheritance, possession. Compare H5158 (נַחַל).

18.gl: to take (in the widest variety of applications)
18.kj: accept, bring, buy, carry away, drawn, fetch, get, infold, [idiom] many, mingle, place, receive(-ing), reserve, seize, send for, take (away, -ing, up), use, win.

18.gl: properly, heed, i.e. purpose; used only adverbially, on account of (as a motive or an aim), teleologically, in order that
18.kj: because of, to the end (intent) that, for (to,... 's sake), [phrase] lest, that, to.

19.gl: an entrance (the place or the act); specifically sunset or the west; also (adverb with preposition) towards
19.kj: by which came, as cometh, in coming, as men enter into, entering, entrance into, entry, where goeth, going down, [phrase] westward. Compare H4126 (מוֹבָא).

19.gl: a sea (as breaking in noisy surf) or large body of water; specifically (with the article), the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river, or an artifical basin; locally, the west, or (rarely) the south
19.dv: from an unused root meaning to roar;

19.gl: to turn; by implication, to face, i.e. appear, look, etc.

19.gl: properly, a standing, i.e. a spot; but used widely of a locality (general or specific); also (figuratively) of a condition (of body or mind)
19.kj: country, [idiom] home, [idiom] open, place, room, space, [idiom] whither(-soever).
19.dv: or מָקֹם; also (feminine) מְקוֹמָה; or מְקֹמָה; from H6965 (קוּם);

19.gl: the shoulder (proper, i.e. upper end of the arm; as being the spot where the garments hang); figuratively, side-piece or lateral projection of anything
19.dv: from an unused root meaning to clothe;

21.dv: (masculine and feminine); from H2690 (חָצַר) in its original sense;

21.gl: properly, the (outer) wall side; hence, exterior; figuratively, secular (as opposed to sacred)

21.dv: or מַקְצֹעַ; or (feminine) מַקְצֹעָה; from H7106 (קָצַע) in the denominative sense of bending;

23.gl: a wall; hence, a fortress or a hamlet

23.dv: from an unused root meaning to range in a regular manner;

24.kj: in it, [phrase] thence, there (-in, [phrase] of, [phrase] out), [phrase] thither, [phrase] whither.
24.dv: a primitive particle (rather from the relative pronoun, H834 (אֲשֶׁר));

24.gl: a people (as a congregated unit); specifically, a tribe (as those of Israel); hence (collectively) troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock
properly, to boil up; hence, to be done in cooking; figuratively to ripen
24.kj: bake, boil, bring forth, roast, seethe, sod (be sodden).

24.gl: properly, a slaughter, i.e. the flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice (the victim or the act)
